pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 23 defines PROJECT_DIR='/home/ga/webots_projects/slam_bench'; live test confirmed directory created with correct ownership (ga:ga 755) in check_03_directories.txt

setup_task.sh line 24 CTRL_DIR=$PROJECT_DIR/controllers/slam_logger; export_result.sh line 13 CTRL_FILE uses same path; verifier.py line 51-52 defaults to same path; live test check_08 confirmed file creation and export_result.sh detection at this path

setup_task.sh line 37 rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/slam_benchmark.wbt; export_result.sh line 14 WORLD_FILE uses same path; verifier.py line 53-54 defaults to same path; live test check_08 confirmed file creation at this path with correct export detection

task description says 'controller set to slam_logger'; setup_task.sh creates controllers/slam_logger/ directory; Webots convention requires controller directory name to match controller field value in .wbt

task description says 'DEF SLAM_ROBOT'. Note: verifier.py does NOT enforce this specific DEF name — it only checks for Pioneer3at or Robot node presence. The DEF name is a task requirement but not programmatically verified

task description specifies DEF WALL_N/S/E/W. Verifier line 166 checks regex r'DEF\s+WALL_\w+\s+Solid\s*\{' and requires count >= 4. Wall names are consistent between description and verifier regex pattern

verifier.py line 151 reads this value and checks ts <= max_ts. Task description asks for basicTimeStep 16, which is <= 32. This is an intentional acceptance range, not the exact required value. Verified consistent with verifier logic

verifier.py does NOT check arena dimensions — it only checks for presence of RectangleArena or Floor node (line 161). This metadata value is declared but never enforced by the verifier. Task description asks for 10x8m arena
verifier.py line 172 checks solid_count >= 4. Task description specifies exactly 4 walls. Consistent
verifier.py does NOT check wall positions — it only counts Solid nodes matching DEF WALL_ pattern. This tolerance value is declared in metadata but never used in verification
verifier.py does NOT check robot position — it only checks for Pioneer3at or Robot node presence. This tolerance value is declared in metadata but never used in verification
task description opens with 'You are a field robotics researcher'
SLAM data collection is a core workflow in autonomous systems and field robotics. Appropriate classification

privileged_info_summary: This task requires building a complete Webots simulation from scratch — no pre-loaded world is provided. The agent starts with an empty Webots window and must create two files: (1) a .wbt world file saved to /home/ga/Desktop/slam_benchmark.wbt and (2) a Python controller at /home/ga/webots_projects/slam_bench/controllers/slam_logger/slam_logger.py. The project directory structure (slam_bench/controllers/slam_logger/) is pre-created by setup but empty. The world must contain: a RectangleArena (10x8m floor), four Solid Box wall nodes with DEF names WALL_N/WALL_S/WALL_E/WALL_W at specified positions with Physics and boundingObjects, a Pioneer3at robot (DEF SLAM_ROBOT) at position (-4, 0.1, 0), and WorldInfo with basicTimeStep 16. The Pioneer3at PROTO is confirmed available in the Webots R2023b installation at /usr/local/webots/projects/robots/adept/pioneer3/protos/Pioneer3at.proto. Its EXTERNPROTO URL is https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cyberbotics/webots/R2023b/projects/robots/adept/pioneer3/protos/Pioneer3at.proto. Sensors must be added via the Pioneer3at extensionSlot field (confirmed from PROTO definition line 21). The robot needs: Lidar (name 'scanner'), GPS (name 'gps'), InertialUnit (name 'imu'), and PositionSensors (names 'left_encoder'/'right_encoder'). All these sensor node types are confirmed available in the Webots installation (Lidar.wrl, GPS.wrl, InertialUnit.wrl, PositionSensor.wrl in /usr/local/webots/resources/nodes/). The Python controller must import from the 'controller' module (confirmed importable at /usr/local/webots/lib/controller/python/controller/), use Robot() instantiation, have a while robot.step(timestep) loop, initialize and read all sensors, write CSV to /tmp/slam_log.csv, and call setVelocity on wheel motors. The verifier accepts basicTimeStep values up to 32 (not strictly 16). The verifier does NOT check wall positions, robot position, or arena dimensions — it only checks node presence and counts. Network access is available for EXTERNPROTO downloads.
